title: fix(ui): compact oversized project and cancellation dropdowns
active_goal: Autonomously complete AI Worker Platform GitHub Issue #21 by verifying PR #22 is merged in origin/main, then executing bounded continuation steps in the fix/compact-ui-dropdowns worktree under the master automation prompt until a single draft PR is ready.

evidence: git pull --ff-only origin main fast-forwarded local main to e236580
git log -1 --oneline returned feat(llm): add Agnes provider and reliable Re-Ask composer (#22)
git log origin/main --oneline --grep="Agnes provider" -20 returned e236580 feat(llm): add Agnes provider and reliable Re-Ask composer (#22)
gh pr view 22 confirmed state=MERGED, baseRefName=main, mergeCommit=e2365800ec5a45638bc6d92ba15893b8e23c367d, mergedAt=2026-06-30T16:32:39Z

notes: Selector sits beside compact segmented controls in the Launch Configuration card.
Current shared input class is full-width and does not impose compact select-specific sizing or text truncation.
Selector appears in the Stop Job flow and shares the same input class as the General Item project selector.
Its selected value needs compact sizing without changing the cancellation payload or option values.
Nearby workbench controls already use dense spacing through cockpit-field-group and compact-segmented.
A focused frontend style adjustment is likely sufficient if it preserves width behavior and long-value truncation.

bounded_steps.name: initialize_mission_and_capture_dropdown_discovery
notes: Created the Issue #21 worktree from verified origin/main on fix/compact-ui-dropdowns.
Initialized mission state/log files.
Located both dropdown implementations and documented the first implementation hypothesis without editing frontend behavior yet.

bounded_steps.name: implement_compact_dropdown_hook_and_styles
notes: Added the opt-in compact-inline-select class to the RAN General Item project selector and the cancellation-reason selector in frontend/src/views/HomeView.vue.
Added narrow compact select styling in frontend/src/styles.css without changing the shared cockpit-sites-input behavior for unrelated controls.
Added a focused HomeView regression test to prove the Issue #21 class hook is wired only to the two target selects and verified the full HomeView spec file passes.

bounded_steps.name: run_full_validation_and_partial_browser_uat
notes: Required local frontend validation passed: frontend test, frontend build, frontend smoke, git diff --check, and submodule status review.
Browser verification confirmed the current worktree frontend and backend load on ports 3000 and 8000 without overlays or console errors.
Live browser metrics confirmed the RAN General Item selector renders with the compact-inline-select class at compact dimensions and without page-level horizontal overflow.
MW and RAN Job Detail routes loaded normally in the browser.

Full browser UAT remains incomplete because the live RAN project catalog endpoint fails with RAN_PROJECT_WORKBOOK_MISSING and no active job was available in the current browser-tab session to expose the real cancellation selector.

bounded_steps.name: attempt_live_job_creation_and_browser_session_binding
notes: Attempted to finish live browser UAT by creating or attaching to a current-session active job through the existing UI and APIs.

The in-app browser page-evaluation sandbox does not expose storage APIs, Vue instance handles, or file-input constructors needed to bind a created job into the current browser session through supported automation alone.
No active job exists for the current browser-tab session, so the real Stop Job cancellation selector cannot be rendered naturally without an external-state change.

bounded_steps.name: recover_ran_catalog_environment_and_reclassify_blocker
notes: Classified the reported RAN selector failure as an environment issue caused by an uninitialized submodule checkout rather than a frontend regression.

Ran the one allowed recovery command, git submodule update --init --recursive, which restored skills/create-pr-cd-ran at commit 239910e2816153339a94881597bbb95355059741 and repopulated its config workbook files.
Verified GET /api/jobs/ran-projects now returns HTTP 200 with the real project list, so the live RAN selector data path is available again without any source-code changes.

The remaining blocked acceptance gate is the real current-session Stop Job cancellation selector, because no cancellable active job is exposed in the active browser-tab session and supported automation still cannot bind one through storage or file APIs.

bounded_steps.name: verify_live_ran_selector_and_probe_session_binding
notes: Confirmed the only browser backend available to this thread is the in-app browser surface; no Chrome extension backend is available for a richer file/storage path.

Verified the recovered live RAN General Item selector is enabled, can select the long real project label CD consolidation 2023 (Swap/ Modernize), stays at compact dimensions, and does not introduce page-level horizontal overflow or console warnings/errors.

Native select keyboard-arrow behavior could not be conclusively driven through the in-app browser automation surface, so keyboard navigation for the live RAN selector remains unverified rather than marked regressed.

Created one real MW all-sites job against the most recent historical browserTabSessionId from the database and reloaded Home; the job did not appear in the current tab's Active Jobs list, proving the current in-app tab is not reusing that historical session id.
The remaining blocker is still current-session job binding for HomeView cancellation UAT, not the RAN selector data path or compact-style implementation.

bounded_steps.name: final_in_app_session_probe
notes: Re-probed the current in-app browser tab after a fresh reload for any authoritative session-binding surface exposed to supported automation.

Confirmed the browser runtime exposes no window, DOM, or Vue hooks matching the HomeView session state, including __AWP_HOME_VM__, __VUE__, __VUE_DEVTOOLS_GLOBAL_HOOK__, __vueParentComponent, __vue_app__, or any session/job/AWP-related global keys.
This leaves no supported path in the current iab-only environment to read or replay the active browserTabSessionId required to surface the real Stop Job selector in HomeView.
The resumed goal has now hit the same external blocker for three consecutive continuation turns, satisfying the blocked threshold for the active-goal audit.

blocker.summary: The remaining Issue #21 acceptance gates require a real current-session active job path that is not reachable with the current environment state.
details: The RAN workbook-backed project list is restored and /api/jobs/ran-projects now succeeds after submodule initialization.
No active job exists for the current browser-tab session, so the real cancellation selector is not rendered.

The only available browser backend for this thread is the in-app browser, and its automation surface still does not expose sessionStorage, File/DataTransfer/Blob constructors, or usable Vue app handles needed to bind a created job into the live page through supported automation.
